title: Serapias athensis
taxonomy: Serapias athensis is classified as a species of orchid, belonging to the genus Serapias and the family Orchidaceae. This genus is a group of orchids commonly known as 'bats orchids' or 'bee orchids', characterized by their unique and often striking appearances. Serapias athensis is further categorized as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Serapias athensis is part of the largest and most diverse plant family in the world. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf structure, flower shape, and growth habits.
